title: Chronic pain review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.
examination.rationale: Supports neuropathic pain severity, function, sleep, wellbeing, response and adverse-effect review for chronic pain review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

source.title: Neuropathic pain in adults: pharmacological management in non-specialist settings

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7 — assessment, treatment planning, referral, and review

referral.text: Supports documenting chronic pain, opioid use, medicine history, psychosocial context, alcohol or illicit substance context, function, and specialist involvement.
referral.rationale: Supports chronic-pain, opioid, medicine, psychosocial and functional assessment in perioperative care for chronic pain review; no decision is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports documenting chronic pain, opioid use, medicine history, psychosocial context, alcohol or illicit substance context, function, and specialist involvement.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Pain diagnoses and sites, duration, trajectories, complete medicines, goals, risk, examination, investigations and multidisciplinary plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for chronic pain review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
